integrations.title: Integrations & Tracking
integrations.subtitle: Configure analytics, marketing pixels, and consent management.

gtm.title: Google Tag Manager
gtm.serverContainerHelp: For server-side tracking. Leave empty for client-side only.
gtm.ssEnabledHelp: Send conversion events (Purchase, Lead) from backend directly to GTM SS container. Supports Facebook CAPI, TikTok Events API, and other tags configured in GTM.

facebook.title: Meta (Facebook)
facebook.capiTokenHelp: Access token for Meta Conversions API. Generate in Events Manager → Settings.
facebook.testEventCodeHelp: For testing events in Events Manager. Remove in production.
facebook.capiTokenRequired: CAPI Token is required to enable this feature

consent.sendConversionsWithoutConsentHelp: Send Purchase and Lead events server-side even when user hasn't given cookie consent. Uses legitimate interest legal basis (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f)). Only conversion events are sent - no page views or browsing data.
consent.requiresCAPI: Requires Meta CAPI to be enabled first
consent.legalWarning: Legal notice: This feature relies on legitimate interest basis. Consult your legal advisor for GDPR/ePrivacy compliance in your jurisdiction.
consent.documentsWarning: This feature requires proper Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy that mention server-side conversion tracking.

quickStart.title: Quick Start - Recommended Setup
quickStart.subtitle: Don't know where to start? Follow these 3 steps for optimal tracking.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.desc: Choose how to track page views:
analytics.option2: or GTM + GA4 - more features, needs Google account

marketing.title: Meta Conversions
marketing.desc: Track purchases and leads to Meta Ads:

quickStart.result: After completing these steps you'll have: analytics, conversion tracking, and GDPR compliance. Conversions will work even with adblockers.

gtm.title: Do I need Google Tag Manager?
gtm.whenNeededDesc: GTM is the entry point for the Google ecosystem (GA4, Google Ads). If adblockers block GTM, all Google tracking stops working.

scenario1.title: Meta conversions only (no analytics)
scenario1.desc: Simplest setup. Only tracks purchases/leads to Meta. No page view analytics.

scenario2.title: Meta conversions + Umami analytics
scenario2.desc: Privacy-focused analytics. Umami requires hosting (Umami Cloud ~$9/mo or self-hosted). No Google dependency.

scenario3.title: GA4 + Meta (accept ~15-30% loss from adblockers)
scenario3.desc: Standard setup. Adblockers block GA4 for some users, but Meta conversions still work via CAPI.

scenario4.title: GA4 + Meta + bypass adblockers
scenario4.capi: Our CAPI: Optional (GTM Server can send to Meta)
scenario4.desc: Full tracking. All tags load from your domain. Best data quality. Requires GTM Server hosting.

scenario5.title: Google Ads remarketing
scenario5.desc: GTM Server strongly recommended for Google Ads (better cookies, bypass adblockers, improved conversion tracking).

gtm.serverSetup: How to set up GTM Server?

serverOptions.aws: AWS/Azure - for enterprises, requires DevOps knowledge
serverOptions.vps: VPS (e.g., Mikrus, Hetzner) - cheapest (~$5/month), requires manual setup

gtm.serverNote: After setup, you'll get a URL like https://gtm.yourdomain.com - enter it in 'GTM Server URL' field.

facebook.title: How to configure Meta (Facebook) tracking?
facebook.quickStartDesc: Don't know what to configure? Just follow these 4 steps:

quickStartSteps.step1: Go to Meta Events Manager → Your Pixel → Settings
quickStartSteps.step2: Copy Pixel ID → paste in 'Pixel ID' field below
quickStartSteps.step3: Generate Access Token → paste in 'CAPI Token' field

facebook.quickStartResult: Done! Your purchases and leads will be tracked to Meta. Works even with adblockers.
facebook.quickStartOptional: Optional: Go to Consents tab and enable 'Send conversions without cookie consent' to track conversions even when users decline cookies.
facebook.whatIsDesc: Meta Conversions API (CAPI) sends events directly from your server to Meta, bypassing browser limitations. It works alongside the Pixel for better tracking accuracy and ad optimization.
facebook.step1: Go to Meta Events Manager → Data Sources → Your Pixel
facebook.step3: Copy the Pixel ID and paste it above
facebook.step4: Copy the access token and paste it as CAPI Token
facebook.deduplicationDesc: Both Pixel and CAPI send the same event with matching event_id. Meta automatically deduplicates so you don't get double counting.
facebook.recommendationDesc: For sending conversions without cookie consent, we recommend using our built-in CAPI:
facebook.recommendationNote: Use GTM Server only if you need to bypass adblockers for ALL tracking (including Pixel, GA4) or already have GTM Server infrastructure.
facebook.gtmWarning: Have Meta CAPI tag in GTM Server?
facebook.gtmWarningDesc: If your GTM Server container has a Meta Conversions API tag, make sure it uses the event_id field from dataLayer. This way both GTM Server and our CAPI will send the same event_id, and Meta will deduplicate correctly.

consent.title: How does consent management work?
consent.howItWorksDesc: When consent is enabled, users see a cookie banner. Tracking scripts only run after consent is given. This ensures GDPR/ePrivacy compliance.
consent.legitimateInterestDesc: You can track conversions without consent under legitimate interest if tracking is essential for your business and doesn't override user rights. This is a legal gray area - consult your lawyer.
consent.purchaseDesc: Payment completed - essential for order tracking
consent.leadDesc: Free product signup - essential for lead generation
consent.notAllowed: ViewContent, InitiateCheckout, AddPaymentInfo - NOT allowed without consent

codeGenerator.title: Generate Protection Code

pageInstructions.step2: 2. Paste it into the <head> section of your protected page
pageInstructions.step3: 3. The entire page will be protected automatically

elementInstructions.step1: 1. Add the script tag to your page <head>
elementInstructions.step2: 2. Wrap content you want to protect with data-sellf-product="{slug}"
elementInstructions.step3: 3. Add fallback content with data-no-access for users without access
elementInstructions.step4: 4. The script will automatically show/hide content based on user access

codeGenerator.frontendProtectionDescription: This solution operates entirely on the client-side, making it compatible with any website builder like WordPress, Webflow, Squarespace, or static HTML. While this approach is less secure than server-side protection, we've implemented several obfuscation techniques to make circumvention more challenging than typical frontend solutions.
codeGenerator.pageModeUseCase: Perfect for protecting landing pages, course content, or any standalone page where you want to control access based on user purchases or subscriptions.
codeGenerator.selectiveDisplayDescription: This method allows you to show or hide specific sections of a page based on user access. It's particularly useful for sales pages where you want to display different content to paying customers versus prospects.
codeGenerator.implementationDescription: Add data-sellf-product="{slug}" to content that should be visible to users with access, and data-no-access="true" to fallback content for users without access. The script will automatically manage visibility.
codeGenerator.elementModeUseCase: Replace "Buy Now" buttons with "Access Product" buttons for existing customers, or show premium content sections only to subscribers.

embedInstructions.step1: 1. Paste the code anywhere in your landing page HTML
embedInstructions.step2: 2. The widget will auto-initialize and display a beautiful signup form
embedInstructions.step3: 3. Users enter their email → receive magic link → get instant access
embedInstructions.step4: 4. Perfect for AI-generated landing pages or custom websites

codeGenerator.embedInfoFreeOnly: This embed widget only works for free products (price = $0)
codeGenerator.embedInfoSecurity: Rate limiting (3 req/5min), Turnstile CAPTCHA, disposable email filter
codeGenerator.embedInfoCustomizable: Beautiful gradient design, auto-responsive, works on any website
codeGenerator.embedInfoMagicLink: Users receive instant access link via email - no password needed

form.previewVideoUrlHelp: Video shown on checkout page. Takes priority over image. Best support: YouTube, Vimeo, Wistia, Bunny, Loom.
form.previewVideoUrlUnsupported: Unsupported platform. Use YouTube, Vimeo, Wistia, Bunny, or Loom.
form.setToZeroForFree: Set to 0 for free products. Use comma or dot for decimal separator.
form.productAvailableFrom: Product will be available starting from this date and time
form.productUnavailableAfter: Product will be unavailable after this date and time
form.temporalAvailabilityDescription: Leave both fields empty for permanent availability. Set only "Available From" for products launching in the future. Set only "Available Until" for limited-time offers.

waitlist.enableWaitlistDescription: Show a waitlist signup form when the product is unavailable (inactive, not yet started, or expired).
waitlist.noWebhookWarning: To enable waitlist signup, first configure a webhook for the "waitlist.signup" event.

waitlistWarning.title: No Webhook Configured
waitlistWarning.description: You are enabling waitlist signup, but there is no webhook configured for the "waitlist.signup" event.
waitlistWarning.consequence: Without a webhook, waitlist signups will not be sent anywhere and the collected emails will be lost.

form.accessExpireAfter: When users gain access to this product, their access will automatically expire after this many days. Leave empty for permanent access.
form.autoGrantDurationDescription: This setting applies to automatic access grants (e.g., free products, purchases). Manual access grants through the admin panel can override this setting.
form.redirectDescription: URL to redirect users after gaining access.

postPurchase.passParamsHelp: Appends ?email=...&productId=... to the destination URL. Great for OTO pages!
postPurchase.hideBumpHelp: Appends ?hide_bump=true to the URL. Useful if you want to skip the bump offer in the next step.

refundSettings.infoDescription: Customers can submit refund requests from their purchase history. You will review and approve/reject each request. Admins can always issue refunds regardless of the time limit.

form.controlsTooltip: Show the platform's built-in player controls (play button, progress bar, volume). When off, only the custom overlay is shown.
form.autoplayTooltip: Start playing automatically when the page loads. Browsers require the video to be muted for autoplay to work.
form.loopTooltip: Restart the video from the beginning when it ends.
form.mutedTooltip: Start the video with sound off. Required for autoplay to work in most browsers.
form.preloadTooltip: Start loading the video data before the user presses play. Only supported by Bunny.net.
form.useCustomPlayerTooltip: Use a custom player with thumbnail, overlay controls, and click-to-play. When off, the platform's default embedded player is shown.
form.untrustedPlatform: Untrusted platform. Supported: YouTube, Vimeo, Bunny.net, Loom, Wistia, DailyMotion, Twitch
form.invalidVideoUrl: Invalid video URL format. Please check the URL.
form.untrustedDownloadUrl: URL must be from a trusted provider (AWS, Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive, CDN, etc.)

omnibus.title: EU Omnibus Directive

omnibus.omnibusExplanation: EU Directive 2019/2161 (Omnibus) requires displaying the lowest price from the last 30 days when showing discounts. It has been in effect since May 28, 2022 for all EU customers.
omnibus.currentlyEnabled: Feature enabled - 30-day lowest price will be displayed
omnibus.currentlyDisabled: Feature disabled - price history will not be displayed
omnibus.info1: You can exempt individual products in their settings (perishable goods, new products <30 days)
omnibus.info2: Price history is automatically tracked with every product price change
omnibus.info3: Prices are displayed only when current price is lower than historical lowest

stripeTax.title: Tax & Checkout

taxMode.label: Tax Calculation Mode
taxMode.description: Choose how taxes are calculated for checkout sessions.
taxMode.localDescription: A fixed VAT rate is sent to Stripe for each product. Stripe displays the tax breakdown on checkout and receipts.
taxMode.stripeTaxDescription: Stripe calculates taxes automatically based on customer location and your tax registrations.
taxMode.vatRateHelp: This rate is assigned to newly created products as their default. Each product stores its own VAT rate which can be changed individually. The rate sent to Stripe at checkout is always the product's own rate.
taxMode.noRateWarning: No default VAT rate set. Products created without an explicit rate will have no tax applied in checkout.
taxMode.stripeTaxActiveWarning: Stripe Tax is active on your Stripe account but is NOT used in Fixed Rate mode. Your fixed VAT rate takes priority in checkout sessions. To use automatic tax calculation, switch to Stripe Tax mode.

status.activeDescription: Taxes are being automatically calculated for your transactions.
status.pendingDescription: Tax settings need to be completed in Stripe Dashboard.
status.noPermissionDescription: Your Stripe API key doesn't have permission to read tax settings.
status.stripeNotConfiguredDescription: Configure Stripe API keys first to check tax status.

toggles.taxIdCollectionDescription: Allow customers to provide their tax ID (e.g., VAT number) during checkout.
toggles.billingAddressDescription: Whether billing address is optional or required during checkout.
toggles.expiresHoursDescription: How many hours a checkout session remains valid (1–168).
toggles.collectTermsDescription: Require customers to accept Terms of Service during checkout.

step1.title: Secure Stripe Integration
step1.subtitle: Configure your Stripe API keys using Restricted API Keys (RAK) for maximum security

whatIsRAK.title: What are Restricted API Keys?
whatIsRAK.description: Restricted API Keys (RAK) allow you to grant specific permissions to your application, following the principle of least privilege. Instead of using full access keys, you only grant the permissions Sellf actually needs.
whatIsRAK.recommendedText: for self-hosted integrations and production environments.

encrypted.title: Encrypted Storage
encrypted.description: Your API keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before being stored in the database.

guided.title: Guided Setup
guided.description: Step-by-step instructions with visual guides to create your Restricted API Key in Stripe.

validation.title: Automatic Validation
validation.description: We automatically test your key and verify it has all required permissions.

rotation.title: Key Rotation Tracking
rotation.description: Tracks when your key was created so you know when to rotate it (recommended every 90 days).

alternative.title: Alternative: .env Configuration
alternative.description: Developers can also configure Stripe by setting STRIPE_SECRET_KEY directly in .env file. Both methods are equally supported.

step2.title: Choose Environment Mode
step2.subtitle: Select whether you want to configure Test or Live mode

test.title: Test Mode
test.description: Use test mode to safely practice and verify your integration without processing real payments.

live.title: Live Mode
live.description: Use live mode for your production environment to accept real payments from customers.

info.title: You can configure both later
info.description: You can set up both Test and Live mode separately. Start with Test mode to practice, then add Live mode when ready for production.

step3.title: Create Your Restricted API Key
step3.subtitle: Follow these steps to create a secure Restricted API Key in Stripe

openDashboard.title: Step 1: Open Stripe Dashboard
openDashboard.description: Click below to open the API Keys page in a new tab

step2.title: Create a Restricted Key
step2.description: In the Stripe dashboard, click "Create restricted key" button (not "Create secret key")

step3.title: Name Your Key
step3.description: Give your key a descriptive name, for example:

step4.title: Set Required Permissions
step4.description: Configure the following permissions for Sellf to work properly:
step4.importantText: Required permissions are marked with ⚠️. Without these, Sellf will not function properly.

step5.title: Create & Copy the Key
step5.description: Click "Create key" and immediately copy it. You will paste it in the next step.
step5.warning: The key is only shown once! Make sure to copy it.

step4.title: Enter Your API Key
step4.subtitle: Enter your Stripe API Key (Restricted or Secret Key)

keyTypeInfo.title: Choose Your Key Type
keyTypeInfo.restricted: More secure — only grants permissions Sellf needs. Recommended for production.
keyTypeInfo.secret: Full API access. Easier to set up, but less secure if leaked. Fine for development.

input.label: Stripe API Key
input.help: Your key will be encrypted with AES-256-GCM before being stored.

permissions.title: Missing Required Permissions
permissions.help: Go back to Stripe and update your key permissions.

success.title: API Key Validated Successfully!
success.description: Your key has been verified and all required permissions are granted.
success.descriptionSecret: Your Secret Key has been verified with full API access. All payment functionality is now available.
success.descriptionRestricted: Your Restricted Key has been verified with all required permissions.

step5.title: Configuration Complete!
step5.subtitle: Your Stripe integration is now configured and ready to use

security.title: Encrypted & Secure
security.description: Your API key has been encrypted with AES-256-GCM and securely stored in the database.

rotation.title: Key Rotation
rotation.description: We recommend rotating your API key every 90 days as a security best practice. You can do this anytime from Settings.

hero.metaDescription: Sell digital products on your own terms. Self-hosted, secure, and fully yours. No monthly fees, no middlemen.

hero.subtitleBottom: No monthly fees. No middlemen. Complete control from day one.

payments.title: Accept payments everywhere
payments.desc: Customers pay without leaving your page. 20+ currencies, guest checkout, bank-level encryption.

coupons.title: Run promos that sell

webhooks.title: Connect with your tools
webhooks.desc: Connect Sellf to Zapier, Make, or any tool. Automatic notifications after every sale, with retries on failure.

leads.title: Build your audience for free

delivery.title: Deliver content your way
delivery.desc: Serve files for download directly from Sellf, or redirect buyers to your course platform.

omnibus.title: Stay EU-compliant automatically
omnibus.desc: Sellf automatically tracks price history as required by the Omnibus Directive. Zero manual work.

saleLimits.title: Create real urgency
saleLimits.desc: Set time or quantity limits. Live countdowns motivate faster purchases.

funnels.title: Turn buyers into repeat customers
funnels.desc: After purchase, offer the next product or a return coupon. Build a path from first-time buyer to loyal customer.

refunds.title: Handle refunds without headaches
refunds.desc: Set refund rules separately for each product. Customer fills a form, you approve with one click.

security.title: Your keys never leave your server
security.desc: Stripe credentials stay in your .env file. No telemetry, no external calls. RLS on every table, penetration-tested, with a built-in security audit panel.

tax.taxGrowthTitle: Tax compliance grows with your business
tax.taxStep1Desc: Below €10,000 EU cross-border sales. Handle VAT in your own country only
tax.taxStep2Desc: Above €10,000. Register for EU OSS (one form) and add Stripe Tax (+0.5%) for automatic VAT calculation
tax.taxStep3Desc: Optional: Stripe Managed Payments (Stripe as MoR) or a tax accountant for full compliance
tax.taxDisclaimer: This is general information, not tax or legal advice. Tax obligations vary by country and business type. Consult a qualified tax professional for your specific situation.

courses.title: Online Courses

digital.title: Digital Products

leads.title: Free Products & Sales Funnels

donate.title: Support Open Source
donate.subtitle: Sellf is free and always will be. If it saves you money, consider supporting development.
donate.note: Every contribution helps us ship new features faster.

items.q: Is it really free? What's the catch?
items.a: Yes. The software is free forever (MIT license). You pay only for hosting (~$3/mo and up) and Stripe processing (~2.9%). No platform fees, no subscriptions, no lock-in.

items.q: I already use Gumroad. Why should I switch?
items.a: Gumroad takes 10% per sale. Teachable charges $39-665/mo. Sellf is free - you self-host it, own the code and data. The only cost is Stripe's processing fee.

items.q: I'm not a developer. Can I still set this up?
items.a: Yes. If you can follow step-by-step instructions and type a few SSH commands, you can deploy Sellf. We provide Docker configs and detailed guides.

items.q: How much will I actually pay per sale?
items.a: Only Stripe's processing fee: ~2.9% + 30¢ per transaction. No platform fees on top. MoR platforms charge 5-10% total - significantly more.

items.q: Can I customize it to match my brand?
items.a: Yes. MIT license - change everything: branding, colors, domain, code. It's fully your platform.

items.q: Is it secure enough for real customers?
items.a: Yes. Regularly penetration-tested (280+ test cases, OWASP Top 10). RLS on every database table, AES-256 key encryption, HMAC webhooks, secure-by-default CORS. Your Stripe keys never leave your server. Built-in Security Audit panel checks your configuration automatically.

items.q: What if I get stuck during setup?
items.a: Check the docs and GitHub issues. Any Next.js developer can customize or extend it for you.

items.q: Will it handle my traffic if I scale?
items.a: Yes. Next.js 16 + PostgreSQL scale horizontally. Deploy on anything from a ~$3/mo VPS to Kubernetes. Supabase handles connection pooling.

items.q: Do I need a Merchant of Record?
items.a: Most small creators don't. MoR platforms handle taxes but take 5-10% and tie your customer data to their platform. Below €10,000 EU cross-border, you handle VAT only in your country. Own Stripe = full control at ~2.9%. Add Stripe Tax (+0.5%) when you grow.

items.q: How do I handle VAT and taxes?
items.a: Below €10,000/year EU cross-border B2C - handle VAT in your country only. Above that - register EU OSS (one form, all countries). Stripe Tax automates calculation for 0.5%. Consult a tax professional for your case.

finalCta.title: Ready to Start?
finalCta.subtitle: Deploy Sellf in 10 minutes. Start selling without monthly fees.
